name: St. Padre Pio
title: Mystic, Stigmatist, Confessor
quote: Pray, hope, and don't worry. Worry is useless. God is merciful and will hear your prayer.

practices.d: Upon waking, immediately offer your day to God. 'Lord, I offer Thee all my prayers, works, joys, and sufferings of this day.'
Padre Pio's Mass was the centre of his life, often lasting two hours. Attend Mass daily if possible.
Spend half an hour in silent meditation upon the Passion of Our Lord.
Padre Pio called it 'the weapon.' Pray at least five decades each day.
Read fifteen minutes from the Imitation of Christ, the lives of the saints, or St. John of the Cross.
Sit or kneel before Jesus in the tabernacle, in silence.
Before sleep, examine the day's faults and offer them to God's mercy.
Unite every pain and difficulty to the Passion of Christ; 'Co-redemption.'

name: St. Thérèse of Lisieux
title: Doctor of the Church · The Little Flower
quote: Miss no single opportunity of making some small sacrifice, here by a smiling look, there by a kindly word; always doing the smallest right, and doing it all for love.

practices.d: Offer your day as a 'little sacrifice of love' to Jesus. Aspire to please Him in every small thing.
Receive Holy Communion with total trust and childlike confidence.
Recall the mystery of the Incarnation three times a day; morning, noon, and evening.

practices.t: Do Small Things with Great Love
practices.d: Seek out tiny acts of kindness; a smiling look, a kind word, a cheerfully-borne inconvenience.
Receive every small annoyance as a gift from God. Never complain.
Make hidden sacrifices known only to God. Do not seek recognition.
Before sleep, abandon yourself into the merciful arms of Our Heavenly Father, as a little child.

name: St. Thomas Aquinas
title: Doctor of the Church · Doctor Angelicus
quote: To one who has faith, no explanation is necessary. To one without faith, no explanation is possible.

practices.d: Pray: 'Creator of all things, true source of light and wisdom, deign to shed upon the darkness of my understanding the brightness of Thy light.'
Thomas celebrated Mass daily with profound reverence; he wept at the Consecration.
Silent meditation upon a single truth of faith. Simplicity is higher than complexity.
Read slowly from the Summa, Scripture, or the Church Fathers. Study is a form of prayer.
Thomas loved the Rosary for its simplicity; 'all the theology of the Incarnation in 50 beads.'
Adore Christ truly present; the centre of all theology.
Pray the last hour of the Divine Office and retire in contemplative silence.
Ask: 'Did I think of God? Did my study lead me to love Him more?'

name: St. Benedict of Nursia
title: Father of Western Monasticism
quote: Ora et labora; Pray and work. Let nothing be preferred to the Work of God.

practices.d: Benedict's monks rose in the night for Vigils. Begin your day early, before the world demands your attention.
The Divine Office at daybreak. Offer the day's first hours to God.
Sacred reading of Scripture; read, meditate, pray, contemplate.
Work with your hands or mind, but keep your heart fixed on God. Offer each task as a prayer.
Prime, Terce, Sext, None; short interruptions to remember God throughout the day.
Receive every person as Christ Himself. 'I was a stranger and you took me in.'
The Magnificat at dusk. Thank God for the day's gifts and sorrows.
Eat simply, in silence, with a reading from Scripture or the saints.
The Church's night prayer. Ask protection from the terrors of the night.

name: St. Teresa of Ávila
title: Doctor of the Church · Mystic, Reformer
quote: Let nothing disturb thee, nothing affright thee; all things are passing; God never changeth; patient endurance attaineth to all things; whoso God possesseth, in nothing is wanting; alone God sufficeth.

practices.d: Enter the 'interior castle'; turn within to the soul's centre, where Christ dwells.
Receive Our Lord with the deepest recollection.
Read from Teresa's Interior Castle, Way of Perfection, or Scripture.
Meditate on the mysteries. Teresa used the Rosary to steady her soul.
Even in work or conversation, keep the soul gently turned toward God.
Accept every inconvenience with patient endurance; 'patience attains all things.'
Return again to the interior castle. Rest silently in God's presence.
Review the day's faults with humility. Trust in God's mercy.

name: St. Josemaría Escrivá
title: Founder of Opus Dei
quote: Do everything for Love. Thus there will be no little things: everything will be big. Perseverance in the little things for Love is heroism.

practices.d: At the sound of the alarm, jump out of bed immediately. Consecrate the first minute of the day to Our Lord.
'Serviam'; I will serve. Offer the whole day to God through Mary.
The Mass is the centre and root of the interior life. Receive Communion daily.
Spend half an hour in silent conversation with Our Lord.
Work well, for the love of God. Turn every task into prayer. This is Opus Dei; 'the Work of God.'
Pause at noon to remember the Incarnation.
Five decades daily. 'The Rosary is the most powerful weapon to conquer Satan.'
A brief visit in the midst of the day's work, even if only for a minute.
Read from The Way, Furrow, or the Gospels.
Examine yourself on one specific virtue you are working to acquire.

name: St. Francis de Sales
title: Doctor of the Church · Bishop of Geneva
quote: Nothing is so strong as gentleness; nothing so gentle as real strength.

practices.d: Place yourself in God's presence. Foresee what the day will bring and resolve how to meet each thing with gentleness.
Unite yourself with Christ's Sacrifice. Receive Communion with sweet devotion.
A simple, affective meditation on a scene from the Gospels. Speak to Our Lord as a friend.
Gather 'little bouquets' of devout thoughts throughout the day. Return to them often.
Above all, be gentle; with others, with yourself, with circumstances. 'Nothing is so strong as gentleness.'
Francis loved the Rosary as a family prayer; slow, affectionate, meditative.
Review the day kindly. Thank God for graces, ask pardon for faults, resolve to amend.
Read from the Introduction to the Devout Life or the Treatise on the Love of God.
